text: A CUSIP is a 9-character alphanumeric code that identifies a North American security.
An ISIN is a 12-character international securities identifier with a two-letter country code and a check digit.
KYC (Know Your Customer) is verifying a customer's identity before and during onboarding.
AML (Anti-Money-Laundering) rules require firms to detect and report suspicious financial activity.
An IBAN (International Bank Account Number) identifies a specific bank account across borders.
A SWIFT/BIC code identifies a specific bank in international wire transfers.
The debt-to-income (DTI) ratio is total monthly debt payments divided by gross monthly income.
The loan-to-value (LTV) ratio is the loan amount divided by the value of the collateral.
A US ABA routing number is a 9-digit code identifying a bank for domestic transfers.
The price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio is the share price divided by earnings per share.
Earnings per share (EPS) is net income divided by the number of shares outstanding.
APR (annual percentage rate) is the annualized cost of a loan including interest and fees.
Collateral is an asset a borrower pledges to secure a loan, seized on default.
An ETF is a pooled investment fund that trades on an exchange like a stock.
A bond's coupon is the fixed periodic interest payment made on its face value.
A chargeback is a forced reversal of a card payment initiated by the cardholder's bank.
A SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) is filed with regulators when a firm suspects illicit activity.
A CTR (Currency Transaction Report) is filed for cash transactions above a threshold.
MiFID II is an EU regulation governing investment services and market transparency.
Basel III sets bank capital and liquidity requirements to strengthen the banking system.
The office espresso machine needs descaling every month.
The weather forecast predicts rain on Tuesday with highs near 15 degrees.
